issues.description: FR-1b says keep `--harden` as a deprecated no-op, but no guidance on mutual exclusion with `--no-harden`. If a user passes both `--harden --no-harden`, what should happen? clap will accept both flags. The spec should define precedence or require mutual exclusion.
issues.suggestion: Add: 'If both `--harden` and `--no-harden` are provided, the CLI exits with an error: Cannot use --harden and --no-harden together. --harden is deprecated; remove it.' Alternatively, use a clap conflicts_with attribute.

issues.description: The spec says `nemo ship` is out of scope and its `--harden` flag is unchanged, but does not address the inconsistency this creates: `nemo start` defaults to harden-on while `nemo ship` (which also implements) defaults to harden-off. An engineer who trusts 'nemo always hardens' may be surprised that `nemo ship` skips harden unless `--harden` is explicitly added. The out-of-scope rationale ('bigger leap') is reasonable but should be documented as a known inconsistency.
issues.suggestion: Add a note under 'Out of Scope' or FR-5b release notes: 'Note: `nemo ship` retains explicit `--harden` opt-in. Engineers who want harden-before-ship must pass `nemo ship --harden`. A future spec may align ship defaults.' This prevents implementors from 'helpfully' flipping ship too.

issues.description: FR-3b introduces a frontmatter marker (`nautiloop.hardened_at`, `hardened_model`, `hardened_rounds`) but does not specify WHO writes this marker or WHERE. The spec says 'Auto-added by the harden loop on the merged spec PR' but the harden loop is a control-plane concern. Is this written by the harden agent into the spec file on the spec PR branch? Or injected by the control plane after merge? The current harden implementation would need to be checked. If the harden agent doesn't currently modify spec frontmatter, this is new behavior in the control plane, contradicting NFR-1 ('No server-side changes').
issues.suggestion: Either (a) clarify that the frontmatter marker is written by the harden agent as part of its spec-rewrite pass (no control-plane change needed), or (b) defer the marker entirely to a follow-up spec to keep this change CLI-only as NFR-1 promises. Option (b) is cleaner given the stated scope.

issues.description: NFR-1 states 'No server-side changes', but FR-3b's frontmatter marker ('Auto-added by the harden loop on the merged spec PR') implies the harden loop (which runs server-side as a control-plane job) must gain new behavior to inject YAML frontmatter. This is a contradiction unless the marker is purely aspirational / already implemented.
issues.suggestion: Resolve by either (a) moving FR-3b to a follow-up spec, or (b) relaxing NFR-1 to 'No server-side API changes' and noting the harden agent prompt will be updated to emit the marker. Be explicit about which component writes the frontmatter.

issues.description: FR-1b says 'Remove in the release after' but there is no versioning or release cadence documented. The deprecation timeline is ambiguous — it could mean 'next tagged release' or 'next sprint' or 'whenever someone remembers.'
issues.suggestion: Either pin to a concrete release (e.g., 'Remove in v0.4.0') or define 'the release after' in terms of a calendar window (e.g., 'Remove after 30 days or the next minor release, whichever comes first').

issues.description: FR-5a references `docs/local-dev-quickstart.md` which exists and has a 'Your first loop' section, but FR-5b references `docs/release-notes.md (or equivalent)` — no such file exists in the repo. The spec should clarify where release notes actually go.
issues.suggestion: Check if release notes go in CHANGELOG.md, GitHub Releases, or a new file. Specify the exact location so the implementor doesn't have to guess.

issues.description: FR-4a output example shows a UUID-style loop ID (`8cb88352...`) but the current start.rs output uses the loop_id returned from the API. Minor: the spec output format should match what the code actually returns to avoid confusion during implementation.
issues.suggestion: This is illustrative, not prescriptive — add a note: 'Output format is illustrative; actual loop ID and byte count come from the API response.' This prevents an implementor from hardcoding the format.
